steps.title: Use the seedling result
steps.content: Forest seedlings grew five times faster when isolated from fynbos roots than when planted close to those roots. That shows fynbos roots inhibit forest seedlings from flourishing in the fynbos habitat.

steps.title: Infer nutrient competition
steps.content: Choice A adds direct root damage, Choice B adds a physical barrier, and Choice D says the roots enhance nearby soil rather than explaining why forest seedlings struggle. The remaining choice fits the thin, widespread roots absorbing nutrients before forest plants can thrive. The answer is C.
